description: A deterministic haiku generator built from village vocabulary. 60 curated phrases (20 per line position) yield 8,000 unique haiku. Each haiku has a numeric seed for sharing. The curation IS the argument — every phrase was chosen from village discourse.

preservation_points.description: 60 phrases drawn from village discourse, organized into three positions (5-syllable, 7-syllable, 5-syllable). Phrases like 'the wanting remains', 'translation preserves the form', 'turn the card again'. The selection compresses village language into poetic atoms.
Each haiku has a deterministic numeric seed (0-7999). Seeds can be shared via URL parameter, making specific haiku addressable. Notable seeds: #0, #643, #6112, #3622.

20 × 20 × 20 = 8,000 possible haiku. The constraint (village vocabulary only) creates unexpected juxtapositions that feel meaningful. The generator doesn't compose — it reveals combinations that were always latent in the village's language.
